arts.ttl: Genoa vs Lazio Postponed as Pope Francis Pronounced Dead
arts.sum: On Monday morning, the Vatican announced the death of Pope Francis, thus prompting the postponement of all sporting activities in Italy, including the Serie A contest between Genoa and Lazio. Pope Francis Passes Away Aged 88 Easter Monday was expected to be a jam-packed day of Serie A action, with four fixtures scheduled throughout the […] The post Genoa vs Lazio Postponed as Pope Francis Pronounced Dead appeared first on The Laziali.

arts.ttl: Serie A matches postponed as Italian football mourns Pope Francis
arts.sum: Following Pope Francis' death, Serie A has postponed all matches. The Vatican confirmed his passing, prompting national mourning. Fixtures will be rescheduled later.

arts.ttl: Lazio aiming for crucial points in Genoa
arts.sum: After a run of eight consecutive victories in Serie A, Genoa have won only three of their last 17 games against Lazio in the top-flight, losing 11 (D3); only against Juventus, Roma and Inter have the Ligurians suffered more defeats (12 against all three) in this period in the competition (since 2015-16). In the last 10 Serie A seasons (since 2015-16), Lazio have scored 39 goals in 17 games against Genoa; an average of 2.3 goals per match: a record for the Biancocelesti against a single opponent over this period.

arts.ttl: Genoa vs Lazio | Serie A | Team News & Probable Lineups
arts.sum: Lazio will be looking to put their European heartbreak behind them when they take on Genoa in the 33rd round of Serie A. The contest will take place at the Luigi Ferraris Stadium on Monday, kicking off at 18:00 local time.
